commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

options.description:
Compiled: 0 TypeScript, 0 SCSS/Sass
No TypeScript or SCSS files found to compile.
Place .ts/.tsx files in your jsRoot and .scss/.sass files in your cssRoot.

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

options.description:
Compiled: 0 TypeScript, 0 SCSS/Sass
No TypeScript or SCSS files found to compile.
Place .ts/.tsx files in your jsRoot and .scss/.sass files in your cssRoot.
Watch mode started. Monitoring for changes...
JS root:  /tmp/inspectra-help-mvcfrontendkit.cli-1.1.0-5836b487f77749a0af6e6fe38523d0a0/wwwroot/js
CSS root: /tmp/inspectra-help-mvcfrontendkit.cli-1.1.0-5836b487f77749a0af6e6fe38523d0a0/wwwroot/css
Press Ctrl+C to stop watching.

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

commands.name: Development:
commands.description:
1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: watch
commands.description: Compile and watch for changes

1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: Production:
commands.description:
Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.name: Production:
commands.description:
Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.name: init
commands.description: Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings

commands.name: check
commands.description: Validate config and check if assets exist

commands.name: dev
commands.description: Compile TypeScript/SCSS for development (once)

commands.name: watch
commands.description: Compile and watch for changes

commands.name: build
commands.description: Build frontend bundles (production)

commands.name: help
commands.description: Show this help message

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.description: Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings

commands.name: help
commands.description: Show this help message

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.name: Production:
commands.description:
Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.name: init
commands.description: Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings

commands.name: check
commands.description: Validate config and check if assets exist

commands.name: dev
commands.description: Compile TypeScript/SCSS for development (once)

commands.name: watch
commands.description: Compile and watch for changes

commands.name: build
commands.description: Build frontend bundles (production)

commands.name: help
commands.description: Show this help message

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

commands.name: Development:
commands.description:
1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: init

--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: Production:
commands.description:
Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.name: version
commands.description:
Show version information
Run 'dotnet frontend help' for detailed options.
Unknown command: development:

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

commands.name: Development:
commands.description:
1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: Production:
commands.description:
Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:

Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

commands.name: Development:
commands.description:
1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: Production:
commands.description:
Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.name: Production:
commands.description:
Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

- Generates fingerprinted filenames

commands.name: help
commands.description: Show this help message

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

commands.name: Development:
commands.description:
1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: Production:
commands.description:
Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

commands.name: Development:
commands.description:
1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.name: watch
commands.description: Compile and watch for changes

commands.name: build
commands.description: Build frontend bundles (production)

commands.name: help
commands.description: Show this help message

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

commands.name: Development:
commands.description:
1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: Production:
commands.description:
Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.name: version
commands.description:
Show version information
Run 'dotnet frontend help' for detailed options.
Unknown command: production:

2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: Production:
commands.description:
Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.name: version
commands.description:
Show version information
Run 'dotnet frontend help' for detailed options.
Unknown command: production:

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

commands.name: Development:
commands.description:
1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: init
commands.description: Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings

commands.name: check
commands.description: Validate config and check if assets exist

commands.name: dev
commands.description: Compile TypeScript/SCSS for development (once)

commands.name: watch
commands.description: Compile and watch for changes

commands.name: build
commands.description: Build frontend bundles (production)

commands.name: help
commands.description: Show this help message

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

commands.name: Development:
commands.description:
1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: Production:
commands.description:
Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.description:
Show version information

commands.description:
1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: init
commands.description: Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings

commands.name: check
commands.description: Validate config and check if assets exist

commands.name: dev
commands.description: Compile TypeScript/SCSS for development (once)

commands.name: watch
commands.description: Compile and watch for changes

commands.name: build
commands.description: Build frontend bundles (production)

commands.name: help
commands.description: Show this help message

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

commands.name: Development:
commands.description:
1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: Production:
commands.description:
Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.name: version
commands.description:

--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

commands.name: help
commands.description: Show this help message

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

commands.name: Development:
commands.description:
1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: Production:
commands.description:
Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json

commands.name: version
commands.description:
Show version information
Run 'dotnet frontend help' for detailed options.

commands.description: Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings

commands.name: check
commands.description: Validate config and check if assets exist

commands.name: dev
commands.description: Compile TypeScript/SCSS for development (once)

commands.name: watch
commands.description: Compile and watch for changes

commands.name: build
commands.description: Build frontend bundles (production)

commands.name: help
commands.description: Show this help message

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Create frontend.config.yaml with default settings.
--force       Overwrite existing config file

commands.name: check
commands.description:
Validate configuration and check asset paths.
--verbose     Show detailed output
--view <key>  Check a specific view (e.g., 'Areas/Admin/Settings/Index')
--all         Check all discoverable views
--skip-imports  Skip import path validation

commands.name: dev
commands.description:
Compile TypeScript and SCSS files for development (one-time).
Creates .js files alongside .ts files, and .css files alongside .scss files.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: watch
commands.description:
Compile and watch for changes (continuous).
Same as 'dev' but keeps running and recompiles on file changes.
--verbose     Show detailed compilation output

commands.name: build
commands.description:
Build frontend bundles (same as 'dotnet publish -c Release' but standalone).
Useful for CDN workflows where you need bundles before deploying.
--dry-run     Preview bundles without writing files
--verbose     Show detailed build output
Workflow:

commands.name: Development:
commands.description:
1. Run 'dotnet frontend watch' to compile and watch for changes
2. In another terminal, run 'dotnet run' to start your app
Or use 'dotnet frontend dev' for one-time compilation

commands.name: Production:
commands.description:
Run 'dotnet publish -c Release' which automatically:
- Compiles TS/SCSS
- Bundles and minifies all assets
- Generates fingerprinted filenames
- Creates frontend.manifest.json
